benefits: SPEEDS-UP THE DELIVERY OF TREATMENT FOR PATIENTS: AI software allows radiology to carry on out of hours without the radiologist being on site, increasing the number of scans that can be analysed, speeding up the delivery of results and treatment for patients. SPEEDS UP DIAGNOSTIC PROCESS: AI algorithms can interpret CT and MRI scans in seconds, compared to up to 30 minutes for manual review. This speed is critical in stroke care, where every minute counts – especially for treatments like thrombolysis and thrombectomy, which are highly time-sensitive. MORE ACCURATE TREATMENT DECISIONS: Supports the detection of ischaemic and haemorrhagic strokes, helping clinicians make faster, more accurate treatment decisions. REDUCES THE RISK OF SERIOUS LONG TERM IMPACTS: Enables quicker diagnosis so patients can receive treatment faster, reducing the risk of serious long term impacts. SUPPORTS THE NATIONAL OPTIMAL STROKE IMAGING PATHWAY: Supports the National Optimal Stroke Imaging Pathway, ensuring efficient use of radiology resources and reducing duplication. IMPROVES WORKFLOW: AI helps target expert clinical resources where they have the greatest impact, improving workflow and reducing delays.

benefits: The AI strategy aims to improve patient outcomes, enhance clinical decision-making, streamline operations, and optimize workflows. Specific benefits include: 10% reduction in consultation time through Ambient Voice Technology, freeing clinicians from administrative tasks for more direct patient care; enhanced diagnostics through AI-powered imaging and predictive analytics; personalized treatment recommendations; optimized hospital operations through AI-driven scheduling and resource planning; administrative efficiency through automation of routine tasks (reported 10-20% efficiency improvements in pilots); and advanced research capabilities particularly for rare diseases and pediatric genetic disorders.

source_note: The AI Strategy for 2025-2028 outlines GOSH's comprehensive plan to implement AI solutions to improve healthcare delivery for children with rare and complex diseases. The strategy includes three priority areas (enhancing clinical decision making, streamlining operations, optimizing non-clinical workflows) supported by three strategic enablers (building AI infrastructure, creating AI governance, driving collaboration). Implementation will follow a three-phase roadmap: establishing foundations in Year 1, expanding and optimizing in Years 1-3, and achieving full integration by Year 3+.

benefits: The report identifies several benefits realized from AI use: staff productivity (36%), service efficiencies (33%), and cost savings (21%). The greatest AI opportunities were identified in corporate council use such as HR, administration, procurement, finance, and cyber security (88%), health and social care (adults) (49%), and advice and benefits (38%).
source_note: The report is based on responses from 33% of councils. 95% of respondents were using or exploring AI. Generative AI was the most commonly adopted type (83%), followed by perceptive AI (28%) and predictive AI (20%). 68% of respondents were paying external suppliers for AI tools or technologies. Main barriers to AI deployment were lack of funding (62%), lack of staff capabilities (56%), and lack of staff capacity (52%). Main risks identified were cyber security (83%), deep fakes disinformation (69%), and organizational reputation and resident trust (68%).

benefits: RETFound is an AI foundation model in ophthalmology that can identify sight-threatening eye diseases and predict other health conditions including heart attacks, stroke, and Parkinson's disease. It outperforms existing AI systems across complex clinical tasks, works well in diverse populations and patients with rare diseases, and requires only 10% of human labels compared to other systems. The model improves diagnosis of eye diseases like diabetic retinopathy and glaucoma, while also predicting systemic diseases.
organisation: Moorfields Eye Hospital NHS Foundation Trust
owning_team: Moorfields Eye Hospital and UCL Institute of Ophthalmology
source_note: RETFound was developed using millions of eye scans from the NHS and is being made open-source and freely available on GitHub for any institution worldwide. It was trained on 1.6 million images from Moorfields Eye Hospital using AI tools provided by INSIGHT. The research was published in Nature and led by Yukun Zhou and Professor Pearse Keane.

benefits: The Global RETFound initiative aims to develop the first globally representative AI foundation model in medicine using 100 million eye images collected from over 65 countries. This model addresses bias in healthcare AI by creating one of the most geographically and ethnically diverse medical datasets. It will be evaluated across multiple ophthalmic and systemic diseases including diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, age-related macular degeneration and cardiovascular disease. The model will be released under a Creative Commons license, making it freely available for non-commercial research worldwide.
organisation: Moorfields Eye Hospital NHS Foundation Trust
owning_team: Research consortium led by Moorfields Eye Hospital NHS Foundation Trust, University College London (UCL), National University of Singapore Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, and the Chinese University of Hong Kong
source_note: The initiative builds on the success of RETFound, the first foundation model for retinal and systemic disease detection published in Nature in 2023. The Global RETFound Consortium uses a flexible, two-pronged data sharing framework to accommodate varying technical capacities and regulatory requirements across participating institutions. The project is supported by NIHR Moorfields Biomedical Research Centre and Moorfields Eye Charity.

source_note: This report examines radiology AI (most prevalent), ambient AI (minimal usage), and summary AI (no reported usage) across NHS trusts. It identifies 38 different AI vendors with applications in stroke detection, chest imaging, cardiovascular imaging, fracture detection, dermatology, and radiotherapy. The analysis concludes that while AI adoption is growing, human oversight remains essential, and the true value of AI may lie in enhancing reporting quality rather than replacing clinicians.

benefits:
Time: AI can instantly generate a structured first draft based on inspection evidence, eliminating the need to start from scratch and reducing time spent on formatting. Less time spent writing reports means more time for the evaluative process or more reports produced in the same amount of time.
Cost: Inspector's time-savings can be expressed in financial terms but are non-cash-releasing.
Quality and consistency: A report's first draft will be of greater quality as consistent styling and consideration for what makes a good draft report is applied universally. Improved quality and consistency will in turn increase clarity, ease of quality assurance, and trust.
source_note: The AI Writing Assistant tool is designed to help Ofsted inspectors draft children's home inspection reports by providing suggested wording for 3 specific sections based on inspection evidence. It uses a ChatGPT 4.1 Mini model running on internal Azure instances with a Python pipeline that processes PDF documents of inspection evidence. The tool is currently in Private Beta phase with a limited number of test users, and if implemented fully would run several hundred times per week. The tool does not make judgments about children's homes and all outputs are reviewed, edited and quality assured by human inspectors, maintaining them as the ultimate decision-makers.

Another benefit for customers is allowing them to choose communication method.
organisation: Children and Family Court Advisory and Support Service
source_note: The Genesys Dialog Engine Bot Flows and Genesys Digital Bot Flows uses a natural language understanding (NLU) engine that can interpret and process information the customer provides as input. The system handles approximately 2,500 queries per month with a query answer success rate of 64% based on user satisfaction.

benefits: Camden's rent teams have struggled to focus their efforts on the right areas. source_note: RentSense employs algorithms to identify payment patterns and payment types from rent transaction data to predict which tenants may or may not pay their rent. It highlights any decline in payments which could suggest financial difficulties. The system presents housing officers with a prioritised list of recommended cases for contact.

benefits: The AI tool can spot 95% of cancers according to research. It enables a 'one-day diagnostics' service that could save men up to a month of waiting, reducing weeks of worry and uncertainty. The faster diagnosis pathway could also move care closer to home, with AI-assisted MRI scans potentially being performed in community diagnostic centers. The trial aims to substantially increase the number of patients having suspected cancer diagnosed or ruled out within 28 days, leading to faster treatment and better outcomes for patients and their families. It could also increase capacity for NHS workforce and lead to cost savings.
source_note: The project is one of 7 innovative pilots backed by a £14 million investment aimed to improve the early detection of cancer, as part of the NHS Cancer Programme Innovation Open Call.

benefits: RFR helps identify suspects, missing or deceased people, with police conducting over 25,000 facial searches monthly. Notable successes include identifying violent offenders, burglars, and rioters. LFR has led to over 1,300 arrests in London (Jan 2024-Sep 2025) for serious crimes including rape, domestic abuse, and robbery. OIFR has helped identify vulnerable individuals needing assistance, wanted criminals, and those providing false identities.
source_note: This factsheet from the UK Government provides comprehensive information on police use of facial recognition technology, including types, legal framework, safeguards, and case examples. It notes that 13 police forces were using LFR as of November 2025, and that RFR is widely used across forces. The document addresses concerns about bias and accuracy, with independent testing by the National Physical Laboratory showing 89% accuracy and no significant performance differences based on demographics when used at police settings.

benefits: Enables detection of lung cancer in earliest stages; reaches previously inaccessible lung nodules for biopsy without long delays; uses flexible robotic catheter to navigate deep into lungs; provides faster diagnoses, quicker access to treatment, and improved outcomes while reducing patient anxiety and uncertainty.
source_note: Wythenshawe Hospital is one of the first in Europe to adopt the Ion Endoluminal System, building on the success of the Greater Manchester Lung Health Check programme which has diagnosed over 600 early-stage lung cancers.

benefits: Early detection of lung cancer through a simple blood test using machine learning to identify biomarkers. When lung cancer is diagnosed early, patient survival rate increases tenfold. The test aims to detect lung cancer earlier than CT scans, potentially transforming cancer care pathways, reducing anxiety, improving quality of life for high-risk patients, and resulting in substantial financial benefits for the NHS.
organisation: Oxford University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
owning_team: Oxford University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust, Oxford Radcliffe Biobank, and Oxford Cancer Analytics (OXcan)
source_note: The study will recruit 200 participants to identify cancer biomarkers in blood samples. It runs until December 2025 and uses machine learning software to analyze protein data in liquid biopsies to identify biomarker signatures indicating early-stage lung cancer. The research addresses the critical need for early detection, as lung cancer is the most common cause of cancer death in the UK (21% of deaths) with over 75% of patients diagnosed at advanced stages.
tool_name: Early Lung Cancer Detection Blood Test

benefits: Quicker and more accurate diagnosis of lung cancer, reduced waiting times, increased efficiency, and freed up staff time. With over 600,000 chest X-rays performed monthly in England, this technology supports clinicians with faster diagnosis.

benefits: The service provided by TrustID collects data from people we want to offer a job to. Data subjects have the option of using the digital identity checking service provided by TrustID or they can opt to use a manual checking service.
source_note: TrustID performs biometric liveness and facial recognition checks on a live selfie captured by the person being checked, to confirm they are present at the point of capture and that the selfie is of the same person as the photograph in the document submitted for verification. TrustID also confirms that the document is a legitimate valid UK/Irish passport. Verification that the passport is a legitimate document and that the identity matches that of the candidate provides assurance that the individual does indeed have right to work status in the UK.

benefits: The introduction of Splink increases the speed and accuracy of linkage. benefits: VAIS generates search results with a high degree of relevance, which is the biggest driver for using this search engine product to power GOV.UK site search. This enables users of GOV.UK site search to search using natural language queries (queries that reflect the way that people actually speak) and get highly relevant results. It is effective at handling misspellings or synonyms - so users do not need to know exact government terminology to get highly relevant results.

benefits: The PinPoint Test uses artificial intelligence to analyze blood samples and generate a probability that a patient has cancer. source_note: This three-year programme launched on December 12, 2023, uses advanced AI screening technology to identify patients with long-term conditions who are at greatest risk of needing unplanned emergency care. These patients are then offered targeted, personalized phone-based clinical coaching from healthcare professionals trained in delivering preventative care and self-management techniques. Waltham Forest will be the first borough to receive this initiative through Barts Health NHS Trust and Barking, Havering and Redbridge University Hospitals NHS Trust, with implementation across the rest of north east London (Barking and Dagenham, City of London, Hackney, Havering, Newham, Redbridge and Tower Hamlets) over coming months.
